case_title: US PARKING INC A CALIFORNIA CORPORATION et al VS. CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO A MUNICIPAL et al

docket_entries.description: NOTICE OF MOTION FOR PRELIMINARY INJUNCTION, PROOF OF SERVICE, POINTS AND AUTHORITIES, DECLARATION FILED BY PLAINTIFF US PARKING INC A CALIFORNIA CORPORATION ESREFOGLU, CIHAT AN INDIVIDUAL HEARING SET FOR FEB-10-2009 AT 09:30 AM IN DEPT 302

LAW AND MOTION 302, PLAINTIFFS US PARKING INC., A CALIFORNIA CORPORATION, AND CIHAT ESREFOGLU'S MOTION FOR PRELIMINARY INJUNCTION. ARGUED AND THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: MOTION FOR PRELIMINARY INJUNCTION IS DENIED. PLAINTIFFS FAIL TO DEMONSTRATE THAT THEY ARE LIKELY TO PREVAIL ON THE MERITS. PLAINTIFFS RECEIVED A HEARING BEFORE THE TAX COLLECTOR. PLAINTIFFS' CONTENTIONS THAT ARTICLE 6.18-2 IS INVALID AND THAT THE CITY TAX CODE IS UNCONSTITUTIONAL ARE NOT SUPPORTED BY CASE LAW. SAN FRANCISCO'S SUMMARY JUDGMENT COLLECTION PROCEDURE IS NOT PREEMPTED. POST PAYMENT JUDICIAL REVIEW IS A CLEAR AND CERTAIN REMEDY SATISFYING DUE PROCESS.PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE A FORM OF ORDER. JUDGE: CHARLOTTE WALTER WOOLARD, REPORTER: KENT GUBBINE, CSR #5797

LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO, AND GEORGE PUTRIS'S IN HIS OFFICIAL CAPACITY AS TAX ADMINISTRATOR'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT. ARGUED AND THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: DEMURRER SUSTAINED WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND. PLAINTIFF FAILED TO EXHAUST ADMINISTRATIVE REMEDIES, SAN FRANCISCO BUSINESS & TAX REGULATIONS CODE, ARTICLE 6 SECTIONS 6.13-5 AND 6.15-4. ALSO, THE COURT GRANTS DEFENDANTS' REQUEST FOR JUDICIAL NOTICE. PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE A FORM OF ORDER. JUDGE: CHARLOTTE WALTER WOOLARD, REPORTER: ROSE M. MILLER, CSR #5041

calendar.matters:
CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar May-18-2009 Off Calendar

DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Feb-24-2009 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO, and GEORGE PUTRIS'S IN HIS OFFICIAL CAPACITY AS TAX ADMINISTRATOR'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT. Appearances: Petra M. Reinecke, Esq. of Schwartz & Cera LLP, (415) 956-2600, appearing for Plaintiffs, and opposing party; Peter J. Keith, Deputy City Attorney, CCSF, (415) 554-3908; appearing for Defendants, CCSF, and George Putris in his official capacity, and moving party. Argued and the Court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: Demurrer sustained without leave to amend. Plaintiff failed to exhaust administrative remedies, San Francisco Business & Tax Regulations Code, article 6 sections 6.13-5 and 6.15-4. Also, the Court grants Defendants' request for judicial notice. Prevailing party to prepare a form of order. (302/CWW/JW) Judge: Charlotte Walter Woolard, Reporter: Rose M. Miller, CSR 5041

Notice Of Motion For Preliminary Injunction
Off Calendar Feb-10-2009 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: PLAINTIFFS US PARKING INC., A CALIFORNIA CORPORATION, AND CIHAT ESREFOGLU'S Motion For Preliminary Injunction. Appearances: Petra M. Reinecke, Esq., Schwartz & Cera LLP, (415) 956-2600, appearing for Plaintiffs, US Parking, Inc. and Cihat Esrefoglu, and moving party; Patrick M. Ryan, Esq. of Winston & Strawn, (415) 591-1000, (appearing thru CourtCall) appearing for Plaintiffs U.S. Parking, Inc., and Cihat Esrefoglu, and moving party; Peter J. Keith, Deputy City Attorney, CCSF, (415) 554-3908, appearing for Defendants, CCSF, and George Putris, in his official capacity as Tax Administrator, and opposing party.
Argued and the Court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: Motion for preliminary Injunction is Denied. Plaintiffs fail to demonstrate that they are likely to prevail on the merits. Plaintiffs received a hearing before the Tax Collector. Plaintiffs' contentions that Article 6.18-2 is invalid and that the City Tax Code is unconstitutional are not supported by case law. San Francisco's summary judgment collection procedure is not preempted. Post payment judicial review is a clear and certain remedy satisfying due process.Prevailing party to prepare a form of order. (302/CWW/JW)Judge: Charlotte Walter Woolard, Reporter: Kent Gubbine, CSR 5797
